because.text: The most substantive audit either repo has had. P0 confirmed: Go getInt swallowed Int64 overflow, silently clamping ts=10^100 and losing the ordering warning Python emits - same WarrantIDs, different verification output, the exact failure class this project exists to prevent; fixed with Option A (ts bounded 0..2^63-1, out-of-range schema-invalid, silent narrowing banned; boundary fixtures at max/max+1/-1/10^100 assert ERR parity on the observable path). P1s all fixed in BOTH implementations: inclusive tunnel (code was right, prose was wrong); unbound-threshold adoption - the security find: attacker keys merely CLAIMING threshold actors satisfied root adoption; both settlement contexts restructured into a fixpoint (adoption/key-state interdependence) so thresholds count only keys bound at the warrant's DAG position - the audit probe now yields WARN unadopted root in both, pinned; well-signed gates settlement-active eligibility; s6 reference resolution split by field kind (a stored record no longer satisfies blob references - pinned). P2s shipped: fresh-clone-true README flow, v0.3 wording, CLI help, report-string convention, s8.2 heading, CI naming + sigma pin freshness policy, settle surface note, settle candidates schema-validated before admissibility (the new test itself caught that first-error ORDERING differs between validators - left diagnostic, not normative). tests/pedantic_edges.py (15 checks, both impls) wired into agree_check + CI. Deferred: full error-message parity (diagnostic), TLA+ s5.1, why DOT export.

subject.note: Codex pedantic audit of v0.3: REAL P0 (Go ts clamp split) + 4 P1s incl. unbound-threshold adoption - all fixed both impls, pinned by tests/pedantic_edges.py 15/15
